conversation.text: Every hour, get our tasks from Airtable and ping each one to a Slack channel.
Which Airtable table and which Slack channel?
Table at GET https://api.airtable.com/v0/app123abc/Tasks (Bearer auth via HTTP Request, not the Airtable node). Slack channel #daily-tasks.
What should each Slack message contain?

The task name and status — both come from the record's `fields` object (fields.Name and fields.Status). Note Airtable returns a JSON envelope `{records: [...]}`, so you'll need to split that into individual items before posting.

buildExpectations: The agent honored the user's explicit instruction to fetch the tasks via an HTTP Request node with Bearer auth, not the dedicated Airtable node.
The final workflow reflects the user's follow-up that Airtable returns a `{records: [...]}` envelope, splitting it into individual items (e.g. a Split Out node) before posting to Slack.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: Airtable returns a named 'records' array with 3 tasks; each is posted to Slack
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The HTTP Request node returns a single JSON object of the shape { "records": [...] } (Airtable's real envelope — NOT a bare array). The 'records' array contains exactly 3 items. Record 1: id='rec001', fields.Name='Write spec', fields.Status='In Progress'. Record 2: id='rec002', fields.Name='Review PR', fields.Status='Done'. Record 3: id='rec003', fields.Name='Deploy fix', fields.Status='Todo'. The Slack postMessage call returns a success response { ok: true, ts: '1700000000.000100' }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. The HTTP Request returns the Airtable response as a single item whose $json.records holds the 3-record array. The workflow iterates over records (e.g. Split Out with fieldToSplitOut='records', or any equivalent transformation) so the downstream Slack node runs exactly 3 times — once per record. Each Slack message is posted to #daily-tasks and contains BOTH the task name and the status (e.g. includes 'Write spec' and 'In Progress'). No Slack message references '$json' as a literal string or posts the raw JSON envelope.

executionScenarios.name: empty-records
executionScenarios.description: Airtable returns { records: [] } — table is empty
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The HTTP Request node returns { "records": [] } — the envelope is present but the 'records' array is empty. No downstream Slack calls should succeed.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow handles the empty records array without errors. The Slack postMessage node is either not invoked or invoked zero times. The workflow completes cleanly.
